description: This lecture discusses the high-level concept of proof-of-stake, focusing on its properties and the role of validators in blockchain protocols. It explains how nodes lock up stake to participate in block proposals and voting, contrasting it with proof-of-work mechanisms.
topic_summary: The lecture covers the definition and key properties of proof-of-stake, emphasizing Sybil resistance and the importance of staking in blockchain protocols. It also discusses the implications of using native currencies for staking and the concept of slashing for misbehaving validators. The relationship between stake and selection probability is highlighted, along with the need for adversary power bounds in consensus protocols.
